title: terminalis banksii
intro: Terminalis banksii, also known as the Banks' Rose Madder, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. It is prized for its vibrant red-purple flowers and deep purple roots, which contain the medicinal compound alizarin. The roots of Terminalis banksii have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the roots of Terminalis banksii are often dried and processed into capsules, tinctures, or infusions for use in treating various health conditions.
